text: I feel a curious tension between emptiness and stimulation. The first space is quiet, almost clinical—sterile corridors and the distant glow of emergency signs invoke a sense of detachment. The second space bursts with color, text, and structured information; it demands engagement and suggests innovation. Here, I oscillate between feeling like an observer lost in transition and an engaged thinker ready to absorb new ideas about research and development. Moments like these make me reflect on how environment shapes my emotional disposition and attentiveness.
